title: Exelixis — Financials
html:
Exelixis Financial Architecture

Governing Thought

Exelixis is currently a small-to-mid-size ZoomRx account with episodic revenue tied to specific project engagements rather than annualized tracking contracts. The financial architecture is underdeveloped — no AO plan with brand-level targets has been shared, pricing intelligence is limited, and revenue history is fragmented across a 7-year relationship with gaps. However, the account's financial trajectory is inflecting upward: three zanzalintinib-related engagements in 2026 (campaign impact, ATU RFP, branded campaign testing) signal growing spend, and if ZoomRx wins the live proposals, this account could scale from episodic project work to a multi-service launch tracking relationship worth significantly more. The financial opportunity is directly proportional to how deeply ZoomRx embeds in the zanzalintinib launch infrastructure.

Where to Find Financial Data

System What It Holds Access Update Cadence
Kimble Revenue, project-level financials, effort tracking, invoicing for all Exelixis projects Internal ZoomRx (web app) Real-time as PMs update
Cluster Management (OneDrive) Revenue/Wins/Pipeline by cluster & account; utilization OneDrive, Base 1 file Weekly refresh
SharePoint (Exelixis folder) Project deliverables, proposals, RFP documents SharePoint Project-based
AO Plan [NOT AVAILABLE — no Exelixis AO plan has been shared or documented] -- --

Gap: Unlike J&J and Pfizer, there is no documented AO plan for Exelixis with brand-level revenue targets, contact maps, or growth strategy. This limits financial planning visibility. The account team should request or develop an AO plan as the account grows.

Revenue History

Project Year Service Type Est. Revenue Status
01 EXL_Cabo_PET_2019 2019 PET [Populate from Kimble] Completed
02 Cabozantinib Integrated Tracking 2023 Integrated Tracking [Populate from Kimble] Completed
00 AE Training 2023 Training [Populate from Kimble] Completed
03 Exelixis_Perxcept 2024 Perxcept [Populate from Kimble] Completed
04 Zanzalintinib Unbranded Campaign Impact 2026 Campaign Impact [Populate from Kimble] Active
Zanzalintinib ATU 2026 ATU [Populate from proposal] RFP stage
Zanzalintinib Branded Campaign Testing 2026 Message/Campaign Testing [Populate from proposal] Proposal stage

Revenue pattern: Exelixis revenue has been episodic — a PET in 2019, integrated tracking in 2023, Perxcept in 2024, then campaign impact in 2026. There is no recurring annual baseline. The current pipeline (ATU + branded campaign testing) represents the first opportunity to establish concurrent, multi-service revenue.

Revenue Concentration Analysis

Dimension Current State
Brand concentration 100% oncology (cabozantinib historically; zanzalintinib currently)
Service concentration Shifting from PET/tracking to campaign testing/ATU
Single-franchise risk High — Exelixis is a one-franchise company; all revenue depends on the cabo/zanzalintinib franchise
Number of active projects 1 active (campaign impact) + 2 in pipeline (ATU, branded testing)

Client MR Budget Intelligence

What We Know

Data Point Source Confidence
Exelixis total MR spend Unknown --
Zanzalintinib pre-launch budget Unknown --
Cabozantinib ongoing MR budget Unknown --
Current vendor landscape beyond ZoomRx Unknown --

Critical gap: No visibility into Exelixis's total market research budget, vendor roster, or budget allocation philosophy. This makes it difficult to size the total addressable opportunity or benchmark pricing.

Exelixis Financial Context (Public Data)

Exelixis's corporate financial trajectory provides important context for MR spending expectations:

Metric Detail Notes
CABOMETYX FY 2025 revenue ~$2.4B [UNVERIFIED] If accurate, this is a $2B+ franchise — large enough to support significant MR investment
R&D investment Substantial — multiple Phase 3 trials (STELLAR-301, STELLAR-302) High R&D spend signals willingness to invest in launch preparation, including MR
Company size Mid-cap biotech Budget decision-making may be more centralized and faster than large pharma
Pipeline dependency Zanzalintinib is the primary growth asset Pre-launch spend on zanzalintinib is likely to increase as approval approaches
Revenue concentration CABOMETYX is ~100% of commercial revenue All MR budgets trace back to the oncology franchise

Implication for MR budgets: A $2B+ franchise company in pre-launch for its successor product is in the highest-spending phase of the MR lifecycle. Exelixis is likely investing heavily in:

  • Pre-launch market understanding (ATU — hence the RFP)
  • Message development and testing (branded/unbranded campaign work — hence the active projects)
  • KOL and field force strategy (MSL/MEE, PET — not yet engaged with ZoomRx)
  • Launch readiness assessment (Launch KPI — a future opportunity)

Action items:

  • Pull Kimble revenue history to establish trailing revenue and margin data
  • Review ATU RFP document for any budget signals (budget range, SOW structure, evaluation criteria)
  • Research Exelixis investor presentations or annual reports for MR budget commentary
  • During next client interaction, explore budget appetite for multi-service engagements

Pricing Architecture

Pricing Context

No preferred vendor discount has been established with Exelixis. As a mid-cap biotech, Exelixis may be:

  • More price-sensitive than large pharma (J&J, Pfizer) on per-project pricing
  • More willing to invest in services they perceive as launch-critical
  • Less structured in procurement — decisions may be faster and more directly tied to insights team preferences

Observed / Estimated Pricing

Service Estimated Price Range Notes
PET (historical) [Populate from Kimble — 2019 Cabo PET] Oncology PET benchmarks suggest $75K-$150K per brand
ATU [Populate from RFP/proposal] Standard ATU pricing: $50-75K; may be higher for oncology-specific design
Campaign Impact (unbranded) [Populate from active SOW] Multi-wave design suggests per-wave or annual pricing
Branded Campaign Testing [Populate from proposal v0.2] Message testing typically $50-100K depending on scope
Integrated Tracking [Populate from Kimble — 2023 project] Likely $100-200K range for multi-component tracking

Pricing Heuristics for Exelixis

  • Launch-critical work commands premium pricing. Zanzalintinib launch preparation is existentially important to Exelixis — the account team should not underprice services that are tied to launch readiness.
  • Bundling has a natural buyer. Unlike Pfizer's independent brand structure, Exelixis is a single-franchise company. A bundled zanzalintinib launch package (ATU + PET + Campaign Testing + Launch KPI) could be pitched to a single decision maker.
  • Annual tracking SOWs are the goal. Convert project-based SOWs to annual tracking commitments as early as possible — this creates revenue predictability and switching costs.

Revenue Context

Current State: Episodic, Growing

The Exelixis account has historically generated episodic revenue with no recurring annual baseline. The 2026 pipeline represents a step-change: if ZoomRx wins the ATU and branded campaign testing alongside the active campaign impact work, the account moves from single-project to multi-project status.

Growth Scenario

Scenario Revenue Estimate Probability Timing
Base case: Win ATU + campaign impact continues [Populate: campaign impact SOW + ATU SOW] Medium-High FY26
Growth case: Win ATU + branded campaign testing + campaign impact [Populate: all three SOW values] Medium FY26
Stretch case: Full launch suite (ATU + PET + campaign testing + MSL + Launch KPI) $400K-$700K [UNVERIFIED estimate] Low-Medium FY26-27
Downside: Lose ATU RFP, only campaign impact continues [Campaign impact SOW value only] Low-Medium FY26

Financial Watch Items

ATU and Branded Campaign Testing Proposals (HIGH)

These are live, competitive opportunities. Winning them transforms the account's financial profile from episodic to multi-service. Losing them means another vendor gets embedded in the zanzalintinib launch infrastructure — and re-entry becomes significantly harder.

Action: Treat these proposals as must-win. Deploy senior review on proposal quality. Ensure pricing is competitive but not margin-destroying.

Single-Franchise Concentration Risk (MEDIUM)

All Exelixis revenue traces to the cabozantinib/zanzalintinib franchise. A clinical setback on STELLAR-301 or STELLAR-302 could delay or eliminate the zanzalintinib launch — and with it, the entire pre-launch research budget.

Mitigation: Monitor clinical trial milestones. Maintain cabozantinib lifecycle research as a revenue floor while building zanzalintinib launch infrastructure.

No AO Plan (MEDIUM)

The absence of a documented AO plan means no formal revenue targets, contact maps, or growth milestones for the account. This limits strategic planning and accountability.

Action: The account team should create a lightweight AO plan that covers: FY26 revenue target, key contacts, competitive vendor landscape, and quarterly BD milestones.

Financial Questions to Answer

  • What is the trailing lifetime revenue from Exelixis across all projects (Kimble)?
  • What is the margin on historical Exelixis projects?
  • What is the SOW value of the active Zanzalintinib Unbranded Campaign Impact project?
  • What revenue is implied by the ATU RFP and branded campaign testing proposals?
  • Which cluster owns the Exelixis account and what share of that cluster's revenue does it represent?
  • Has a formal AO plan been created for Exelixis? If not, should one be initiated?

[Populate from Kimble pull and account team debrief.]

Back to Accounts Hub | Home
